article.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle

harvestedTerms.summary: Google and Kaggle announce a free five-day AI Agents Vibe Coding course (June 15-19, 2026) focused on building production-ready AI agents using natural language workflows, tool/API integration, and a capstone project. The article is largely promotional but introduces the framing of '10x agents' and reinforces vibe coding as a pedagogical paradigm for agent development.

article.title: The next phase of the Microsoft OpenAI partnership

harvestedTerms.summary: OpenAI and Microsoft announce an amended partnership agreement covering cloud exclusivity, IP licensing through 2032, revenue share arrangements, and joint datacenter/silicon collaboration. The article touches on strategic partnership structure, cloud infrastructure, and next-gen silicon development.

article.title: An open-source spec for Codex orchestration: Symphony.

harvestedTerms.summary: OpenAI's Symphony article describes an open-source orchestration spec that turns a project-management board (Linear) into a control plane for coding agents, enabling always-on, parallel, multi-agent software development workflows. Key novel concepts include task-tracker-as-control-plane orchestration, DAG-based task dependency execution, and continuous agent supervision at the ticket level rather than session level.

article.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents

harvestedTerms.summary: Article about Choco's use of OpenAI APIs to build AI agents (OrderAgent, VoiceAgent) for food distribution automation, covering multimodal order processing, in-context learning, evaluation frameworks, and the emerging role of non-engineer 'agent orchestrators'.
